subject: [PATCH v2] mm/mm_init: handle alloc_percpu failure in free_area_init_core_hotplug

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand (Arm)
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the patch and gave an Acked-by tag.

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Applied the patch to the fixes branch of the memblock.git tree.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, 01 Jul 2026 18:16:13 -0400, Gregory Price wrote:
> We miss a failed allocation check for pgdat->per_cpu_nodestats, which
> results in a NULL deref when we offset into the per-cpu area.
> 
> Propagate -ENOMEM up the stack and leave per_cpu_nodestats pointing
> at boot_nodestats so a later online can retry the allocation.
> 
> hotadd_init_pgdat() returns NULL on failure, which __try_online_node()
> already maps to -ENOMEM.
> 
> [...]

Applied to fixes branch of memblock.git tree, thanks!

[1/1] mm/mm_init: handle alloc_percpu failure in free_area_init_core_hotplug
      commit: 2ebce860bdd7ae5e13002811bc9bbbf33fcfc221

tree: https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/rppt/memblock
branch: fixes

--
Sincerely yours,
Mike.

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch addresses a memory hotplug issue where the allocation of per-cpu node statistics fails, leading to a NULL dereference when trying to access them. The patch propagates the -ENOMEM error up the stack and leaves per_cpu_nodestats pointing at boot_nodestats, allowing later online attempts to retry the allocation.
